cataloging_subject.label: Treatises of Fistula in Ano, Haemorrhoids, and Clysters
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The cataloged unit is the ten-part Middle English compilation of John Arderne's medical writings edited by D'Arcy Power: principally the anonymous translation in British Library Sloane MS 6, with two lacunae supplied in 1910 from the different English version in Sloane MS 277. It is distinct from Arderne's underlying Latin treatises, each physical witness, Power's introduction and notes, the separately sourced charm printed on pp. 103-104, the 1910 and 1968 printed editions as material publications, U-M's 1999 electronic encoding, and the repository XML artifact.

catalog_summary.title: Treatises of Fistula in Ano, Haemorrhoids, and Clysters
catalog_summary.author: John Arderne as author of the Latin source treatises; anonymous Middle English translators
catalog_summary.date: Latin fistula treatise dated 1376; main English translation after 1413 and attested in Sloane MS 6, 1425-1449
catalog_summary.region: England; exact place and dialect of translation unresolved

titles.value: Treatises of Fistula in Ano, Haemorrhoids, and Clysters
notes: This is the established short title of Power's edition and the U-M item, not a claim that the medieval compilation circulated under this exact modern wording.

titles.value: A tretis extracte of Maistre Iohn Arden of fistula in ano and of fistula in oþer placeȝ of þe body and of apostemeȝ makyng fistuleȝ and of emoraideȝ & tenasmon and of clistereȝ: of certayn oyntementeȝ poudreȝ & oyles
notes: Heading at Sloane MS 6, f. 141r, reproduced as the table-of-contents heading in Power's edition.

titles.value: Treatises of Fistula in Ano and of Fistulae in Other Parts of the Body, and of Apostemes Making Fistulae, and of Haemorrhoids and Tenasmon, and of Clysters, Also of Certain Ointments, Powders and Oils

date_statements.display: 1376 for Arderne's underlying Latin treatise on fistula in ano

scope.description: Underlying Latin source composition, not the date of the anonymous English translation

date_statements.display: After 20 March 1413; before or during production of Sloane MS 6 in 1425-1449

scope.description: Main Sloane MS 6 English version; Power derives the terminus post quem from an interpolation referring to the late Henry IV

assertion.notes: The manuscript production range is not necessarily the translation date; it supplies only an upper bound for the extant copy.

date_statements.display: Main English compilation attested in the second quarter of the fifteenth century (1425-1449), Sloane MS 6

scope.description: Principal Sloane MS 6 version, excluding Power's later editorial substitutions from Sloane MS 277

places.label: England
notes: Used only at national/cultural scope; no precise translation place or dialect region is asserted.

scope.description: Middle English translation tradition represented by an English-produced witness

assertion.notes: The broad cultural origin does not identify a dialect region or exact place of translation.

language_statements.label: Latin

scope.description: Medical terms, formulas, glosses, and short passages embedded in the Middle English prose

notes: The Latin source works remain a distinct expression; this statement records only substantive embedded Latin in the English compilation.

form_statements.description: The ten-part medical compilation is prose; verse quoted in Power's editorial notes is outside the cataloged medieval unit.

genre_statements.label: Medical compilation
notes: Power states that Arderne's individual Latin treatises were later collected in varying combinations; Sloane 6 presents the ten-part grouping cataloged here.

scope.description: Advice on the conduct, presentation, and fees of the surgeon

summaries.value: A Middle English prose compilation of John Arderne's practical medical writings, centered on diagnosis and surgery for fistula in ano and extending to fistulas elsewhere, mormales, haemorrhoids, tenesmus, prolapse, clysters, powders, oils, waters, and compound remedies. The work combines procedural instruction, case histories, recipes, surgical instruments, prognosis, and advice on professional conduct.

The Sloane 6 table and Power's edition organize the compilation into ten sections containing 44 topics. The remedy section is textually incomplete in this translation, and Power adds a Latin and English charm after the manuscript text ends abruptly.

The familiar author label 'John Arderne' identifies the Latin source author. The Middle English wording is anonymous, and Power's printed text is composite because pages 20-23 and 26-29 replace losses in Sloane 6 with another English version from Sloane 277.

extent.description: Ten core medical sections and 44 numbered topics in the Sloane 6 table and Power edition. Counts exclude Power's front matter, separately sourced charm, appendix, notes, and index; they are intellectual/encoding structure metrics rather than a claim that every manuscript has identical divisions.

content_parts.label: Fistula in ano
content_parts.summary: Six topics on the surgeon, instruments, causes, definition, prognosis, operative techniques, after-treatment, and difficult anal fistulas.
notes: Power supplies two substantial gaps within this section from Sloane MS 277 on pp. 20-23 and 26-29.

content_parts.label: Fistula in the limbs
content_parts.summary: Seven topics on fistulas and related swellings or wounds in fingers, joints, legs, knees, feet, ankles, arms, and shin bones.

content_parts.label: Of Mormales
content_parts.summary: One topic defining and treating mormales, chronic ulcerative lesions of the legs.

content_parts.label: Of Piles
content_parts.summary: One topic on haemorrhoids and their treatment.
notes: The U-M encoding header warns that the table of contents and main text disagree around the transition from mormales to piles and that a supplied heading was inserted.

content_parts.label: Of Tenesmus
content_parts.summary: One topic on tenesmus and anal fissures and their treatment.

content_parts.label: Of Prolapse
content_parts.summary: One topic on rectal prolapse and its treatment.

content_parts.label: Of Clysters
content_parts.summary: One treatise on clysters, their apparatus, ingredients, administration, and uses.

content_parts.label: Of Powders
content_parts.summary: Nine topics on vitriol, alum, verdigris, arsenic and orpiment, and named medicinal powders or preparations.
notes: Power describes this last remedy treatise as fragmentary in the present English translation compared with fuller Latin material in other manuscripts.

content_parts.label: Of Oils and Waters
content_parts.summary: Thirteen topics giving recipes and properties for oils, ointments, waters, plasters, and plant preparations.

content_parts.label: Of Valences
content_parts.summary: Four topics on preparations of scabious and wormwood and on inducing and reversing sleep.
notes: Sloane MS 6 ends abruptly after topic 44 on f. 174v; Power's charm on pp. 103-104 comes from an unidentified different source and is outside this core part.

related_works.title: Practica de fistula in ano and associated Latin medical treatises
notes: Arderne issued separate Latin treatises that were later collected in varying combinations; this relation does not posit one fixed Latin codex as the sole source of every English section.

evidence.citation: General Corpus / CME source XML, ArderneFistula.xml.
evidence.locator: HEADER; TEXT/FRONT; TEXT/BODY DIV1 nodes ArderneFistula:6-16; TEXT/BACK
evidence.summary: Identifies U-M item ArderneFistula, the 1968 EETS reprint and 1910 original, Power as editor, Sloane MS 6 as principal source, Sloane MS 277 substitutions, the ten medical sections and 44 topics, page milestones, and U-M's encoding practices.

evidence.citation: John Arderne, Treatises of Fistula in Ano, Haemorrhoids, and Clysters, from an Early Fifteenth-Century Manuscript Translation, ed. D'Arcy Power, EETS Original Series 139 (London, 1910).
evidence.locator: title page; Forewords pp. xii-xv and xxxiii-xxxv; table pp. xxxvi-xxxvii; text pp. 1-102; notes pp. 130-135
evidence.summary: The immediate scholarly edition establishes the title, source-author and editor roles, 1376 date of the Latin fistula treatise, anonymous early-fifteenth-century English translation, ten-part structure, Sloane 6 base, Sloane 277 gap substitutions, fragmentary final remedy material, post-1413 translation terminus, and separately sourced charm.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, item ArderneFistula.
evidence.summary: Current item record identifies Arderne, Power, the Oxford 1968 EETS reprint, the public electronic text and its contents, and U-M's No Copyright-United States statement.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Sloane MS 6, record 040-002112343.
evidence.locator: scope and content; ff. 141-174v; physical description; date; origin; digitised content
evidence.summary: Official item-level description dates the paper codex to 1425-1449, locates its production in England, describes 205 folios, assigns the ten-part John Arderne compilation to ff. 141r-174v, and links the complete digital facsimile.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Sloane MS 277, record 040-002112623.
evidence.summary: Official item record identifies the Arderne Practica de fistula in ano at ff. 60v-75v in Sloane MS 277 and provides the physical-item request route; it exposes no digitised content.

evidence.citation: British Library Sloane MS 6 IIIF canvases for ff. 141r and 174v.
evidence.summary: Visual inspection confirms that f. 141r opens with the 44-topic table headed as Arderne's extracted treatise and that f. 174v contains the close of topic 44; this corroborates the official ff. 141r-174v work boundary and canvas mapping.

evidence.locator: BODY/DIV1[ArderneFistula:6 through ArderneFistula:15] and direct DIV2 children
evidence.summary: Counts ten core DIV1 medical sections with 44 direct topic DIV2 elements (6, 7, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 9, 13, and 4). All core topics are prose; the six encoded verse lines occur in Power's editorial notes outside the cataloged medieval compilation.

evidence.citation: Peter Murray Jones, 'John Arderne's Afterlife in Manuscript and Print,' in Genre in English Medical Writing, 1500-1820 (Cambridge University Press, 2022), pp. 13-31, doi:10.1017/9781009105347.003.
evidence.summary: Current specialist scholarship treats Arderne as the writer behind a Latin and English manuscript and print tradition, emphasizes the illustrated and practical character of that transmission, and points to Jones's earlier study of four distinct Middle English translations.
notes: Only the public abstract and bibliography were consulted; paywalled chapter claims are not used for precise witness dependencies.

open_questions.question: Who produced the main Sloane 6 translation, when and where was it translated, and what dialect profile should be assigned to it independently of the witness's copying place?
next_steps: Consult Peter Murray Jones's full 1989 study of the four Middle English translations and an item-level linguistic description in MEMT or the Linguistic Atlas tradition.
Keep translator identity and dialect region unassigned until directly supported.

open_questions.question: Which manuscript did Power use for the Latin and English charm printed on pp. 103-104 after Sloane MS 6 ends?
next_steps: Check Power's surviving papers or later critical scholarship for an identification of the unnamed source.
Do not bind the charm to Sloane MS 6 or Sloane MS 277 without evidence.

open_questions.question: How much of the final remedy material was absent from the translator's exemplar, omitted by the translator, or lost from Sloane MS 6?
next_steps: Compare Sloane MS 6 with Power's cited fuller witnesses, especially Ashmole MS 1434 and Digby MS 161, using a modern textual study.

notes: The XML header lists Latin, Greek, French, and German, but those labels largely concern embedded terms, quotations, and Power's apparatus. Only Middle English as primary language and substantive embedded Latin are promoted to whole-record discovery languages.
Printed page ranges follow Power's stable pagination as retained in the 1968 lithographic reprint; they are not manuscript folio ranges.
The current BL component description's ff. 141r-174v range supersedes Power's narrower bibliography entry ff. 141-154v for the Practica alone when mapping the full ten-part compilation.
